{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525","id":2706518031,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6hUjAP","number":5525,"title":"`--show-errors` is not enabled by default and does not generate valid JSON","user":{"login":"dylon","id":516519,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxNjUxOQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/516519?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon","html_url":"https://github.com/dylon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-30T02:52:43Z","updated_at":"2024-12-02T00:05:19Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"`--show-errors` is not enabled by default but should be because it is required for the LSP integration. To enable it, you must install rapidjson and then pass the flag `-DWITH_JSON=yes` to cmake.

Also, something has changed that now makes it respond with invalid JSON. If I introduce an error to `tests/function_call1.f90` and call `--show-errors` on it, I get the following:

```shell
./src/bin/lfortran --show-errors tests/function_call1.f90
```

```json
{"uri":"uri","diagnostics":["source":"lpyth","range":{"start":{"line":20,"character":1},"end":{"line":20,"character":5}},"message":"Statement or Declaration expected inside program, found Variable name","severity":0}]}
```

This is invalid for the following reason:

```
parse error: ':' not as part of an object at line 1, column 37
```

If you look at the first element of the `"diagnostics"` field, it wants to treat the array as an object and uses field initialization syntax. It looks like something changed to leave off the opening and closing curly braces for the elements of the diagnostics array.

Here is the text with the error I introduced (noticed the literal term, `error`, at the end):

```fortran
module module_function_call2
    type :: softmax
    contains
      procedure :: eval_1d
    end type softmax
  contains
  
    pure function eval_1d(self, x) result(res)
      class(softmax), intent(in) :: self
      real, intent(in) :: x(:)
      real :: res(size(x))
    end function eval_1d
  
    pure function eval_1d_prime(self, x) result(res)
      class(softmax), intent(in) :: self
      real, intent(in) :: x(:)
      real :: res(size(x))
      res = self%eval_1d(x)
    end function eval_1d_prime
end module module_function_call1
error
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2510318398","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525#issuecomment-2510318398","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525","id":2510318398,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6VoGs-","user":{"login":"dylon","id":516519,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxNjUxOQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/516519?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon","html_url":"https://github.com/dylon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-02T00:05:18Z","updated_at":"2024-12-02T00:05:18Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Addressed by this PR: https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5543","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2510318398/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

To enable it, you must install rapidjson and then pass the flag `-DWITH_JSON=yes` to cmake.

Also, something has changed that now makes it respond with invalid JSON. If I introduce an error to `tests/function_call1.f90` and call `--show-errors` on it, I get the following:

```shell
./src/bin/lfortran --show-errors tests/function_call1.f90
```

```json
{"uri":"uri","diagnostics":["source":"lpyth","range":{"start":{"line":20,"character":1},"end":{"line":20,"character":5}},"message":"Statement or Declaration expected inside program, found Variable name","severity":0}]}
```

```
parse error: ':' not as part of an object at line 1, column 37
```

If you look at the first element of the `"diagnostics"` field, it wants to treat the array as an object and uses field initialization syntax. It looks like something changed to leave off the opening and closing curly braces for the elements of the diagnostics array.

Here is the text with the error I introduced (noticed the literal term, `error`, at the end):

```fortran
module module_function_call2
    type :: softmax
    contains
      procedure :: eval_1d
    end type softmax
  contains
  
    pure function eval_1d(self, x) result(res)
      class(softmax), intent(in) :: self
      real, intent(in) :: x(:)
      real :: res(size(x))
    end function eval_1d
  
    pure function eval_1d_prime(self, x) result(res)
      class(softmax), intent(in) :: self
      real, intent(in) :: x(:)
      real :: res(size(x))
      res = self%eval_1d(x)
    end function eval_1d_prime

```console
% npx vsce package     
Executing prepublish script 'npm run vscode:prepublish'...

> lfortran-lsp@1.0.0 vscode:prepublish
> npm run package


> lfortran-lsp@1.0.0 package
> npm run check-types && node esbuild.js --production


> lfortran-lsp@1.0.0 check-types
> tsc --noEmit

(node:78135) Warning: To load an ES module, set "type": "module" in the package.json or use the .mjs extension.
(Use `node --trace-warnings ...` to show where the warning was created)
/Users/pranavchiku/repos/lfortran-lsp/esbuild.js:1
import * as esbuild from 'esbuild';
^^^^^^

SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module
    at internalCompileFunction (node:internal/vm:73:18)
    at wrapSafe (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1153:20)
    at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1205:27)
    at Module._extensions..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1295:10)
    at Module.load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1091:32)
    at Module._load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:938:12)
    at Function.executeUserEntryPoint [as runMain] (node:internal/modules/run_main:83:12)
    at node:internal/main/run_main_module:23:47
```

use the following diff:

```diff
diff --git a/esbuild.js b/esbuild.js
index 9599ae0..d447f02 100644
--- a/esbuild.js
+++ b/esbuild.js
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-import * as esbuild from 'esbuild';
+const esbuild = require('esbuild');
 
 const watch = process.argv.includes('--watch');

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran-lsp/pulls/comments/1895126782","pull_request_review_id":2519747908,"id":1895126782,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOM4tkcc5w9Vb-","diff_hunk":"@@ -63,6 +63,12 @@ "default": "lfortran", "description": "The path to the LFortran compiler executable." }, + "LFortranLanguageServer.compiler.flags": {","path":"package.json","commit_id":"5daf4b07d12e9ba5a721fe5f80cb0c817efabf26","original_commit_id":"5daf4b07d12e9ba5a721fe5f80cb0c817efabf26","user":{"login":"dylon","id":516519,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxNjUxOQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/516519?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon","html_url":"https://github.com/dylon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"This will need to be an array since the executed command is not a concatenated string. `lfortran` is invoked by its path and the arguments for it are passed as an array. If the flags are space-separated then they will be treated as a single argument which will almost certainly cause a failure. I will submit a PR against this branch with the fix. VSCode handles arrays in its configuration oddly. In my local feature branch, I've added the option to specify additional include paths but the added paths do not appear in the configuration UI. new `--rename-symbol` option sometimes return the wrong ranges for symbols. For example, when I want to rename `eval_1d` in `tests/function_call1.f90`, lfortran gives me the following ranges:

```shell
./src/bin/lfortran --rename-symbol --line=18 --column=22 tests/function_call1.f90 | jq
```

```json
[
  {
    "kind": 1,
    "location": {
      "range": {
        "start": {
          "character": 5,
          "line": 8
        },
        "end": {
          "character": 25,
          "line": 12
        }
      },
      "uri": "uri"
    },
    "name": "eval_1d"
  },
  {
    "kind": 1,
    "location": {
      "range": {
        "start": {
          "character": 7,
          "line": 4
        },
        "end": {
          "character": 28,
          "line": 4
        }
      },
      "uri": "uri"
    },
    "name": "eval_1d"
  }
]
```

If we look at the text of [the file](https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/blob/main/tests/function_call1.f90):

```fortran
module module_function_call1
    type :: softmax
    contains
      procedure :: eval_1d
    end type softmax
  contains
  
    pure function eval_1d(self, x) result(res)
      class(softmax), intent(in) :: self
      real, intent(in) :: x(:)
      real :: res(size(x))
    end function eval_1d
  
    pure function eval_1d_prime(self, x) result(res)
      class(softmax), intent(in) :: self
      real, intent(in) :: x(:)
      real :: res(size(x))
      res = self%eval_1d(x)
    end function eval_1d_prime
end module module_function_call1
```

, the correct ranges should be the following:
1. start: line=3, character=19; end: line=3, character=26
2. start: line=7, character=18; end: line=7, character=25
3. start: line=11, character=17; end: line=11, character=24
4. start: line=17, character=17; end: line=17, character=24","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5524/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5524/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"reopened"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2558848741","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5524#issuecomment-2558848741","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5524","id":2558848741,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6YhO7l","user":{"login":"dylon","id":516519,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxNjUxOQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/516519?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon","html_url":"https://github.com/dylon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-23T03:44:55Z","updated_at":"2024-12-23T03:44:55Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"It misses the symbol where it is used as a member reference and returns the whole declaration of the procedure. end module module_function_call1

- Opens the issue reporter to submit a generated bug report when an internal error is detected.
- Uses `lfortran` to rename symbols instead of the internal, global replacement logic.

At the moment, there is only one heuristic for detecting errors when `--rename-symbol` yields results that make the document erroneous when applied. If this looks good, we will need to create many more bug report providers for the various types of internal errors we want to monitor.

- Addresses issue #55 by jumping to symbol definitions in external documents.
- Resolves the canonical paths of includes.
- Corrects off-by-one errors on changes to the line numbers from lfortran.
- Adds support for matching completions against all symbols in the document.
- Corrects the completion kinds by mapping them from type SymbolKind to type CompletionItemKind.
- Corrects the mtime type of FileCacheEntry to Date; resolves file paths while extracting definitions.
- Corrects highlighting and query extraction logic by matching against type strings in additional to symbol literals.
- Depends on lfortran when extracting hover previews.
- Removes LFortranLanguageServer.renameSymbol since it has been deprecated and replaced with the analogous method of LFortranCLIAccessor.
- Corrects unit and integration tests for latest changes.

To understand the changes to text highlighting and query extraction, consider [global.f90](https://github.com/certik/SNAP/blob/master/src/global.f90) from the [SNAP](https://github.com/certik/SNAP/tree/master) repo:

```fortran
!-----------------------------------------------------------------------
!
! MODULE: global_module
!> @brief
!> Global variables for data types, file names and unit numbers, and
!> commonly used numbers
!
!-----------------------------------------------------------------------

MODULE global_module

  IMPLICIT NONE

  PUBLIC

  SAVE
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! Numerical kinds.
!_______________________________________________________________________

  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: l_knd = KIND( .TRUE. )
  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: i_knd = SELECTED_INT_KIND( 8 )
  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: r_knd = SELECTED_REAL_KIND( 13 )
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! File names and unit numbers.
!
! ifile       - input file name
! iunit       - input file unit number
! ofile       - output file name
! ounit       - output file unit number
!_______________________________________________________________________

  CHARACTER(LEN=64) :: ifile, ofile

  INTEGER(i_knd), PARAMETER :: iunit=10, ounit=11
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! Commonly used numbers.
!_______________________________________________________________________

  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: zero = 0.0_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: half = 0.5_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: one  = 1.0_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: two  = 2.0_r_knd

  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: pi = 3.14159265358979_r_knd
!_______________________________________________________________________
!_______________________________________________________________________


END MODULE global_module

```

When the cursor hovers over `r_kind`, all instances of `r_kind` should be highlighted, including those representing `REAL` type suffixes:

#55","user":{"login":"dylon","id":516519,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxNjUxOQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/516519?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon","html_url":"https://github.com/dylon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Changes:
- Addresses issue #55 by jumping to symbol definitions in external documents.
- Resolves the canonical paths of includes.
- Corrects off-by-one errors on changes to the line numbers from lfortran.
- Adds support for matching completions against all symbols in the document.
- Corrects the completion kinds by mapping them from type SymbolKind to type CompletionItemKind.
- Corrects the mtime type of FileCacheEntry to Date; resolves file paths while extracting definitions.
- Corrects highlighting and query extraction logic by matching against type strings in additional to symbol literals.
- Depends on lfortran when extracting hover previews.
- Removes LFortranLanguageServer.renameSymbol since it has been deprecated and replaced with the analogous method of LFortranCLIAccessor.
- Corrects unit and integration tests for latest changes.

To understand the changes to text highlighting and query extraction, consider [global.f90](https://github.com/certik/SNAP/blob/master/src/global.f90) from the [SNAP](https://github.com/certik/SNAP/tree/master) repo:

```fortran
!-----------------------------------------------------------------------
!
! MODULE: global_module
!> @brief
!> Global variables for data types, file names and unit numbers, and
!> commonly used numbers
!
!-----------------------------------------------------------------------

MODULE global_module

  IMPLICIT NONE

  PUBLIC

  SAVE
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! Numerical kinds.
!_______________________________________________________________________

  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: l_knd = KIND( .TRUE. )
  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: i_knd = SELECTED_INT_KIND( 8 )
  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: r_knd = SELECTED_REAL_KIND( 13 )
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! File names and unit numbers.
!
! ifile       - input file name
! iunit       - input file unit number
! ofile       - output file name
! ounit       - output file unit number
!_______________________________________________________________________

  CHARACTER(LEN=64) :: ifile, ofile

  INTEGER(i_knd), PARAMETER :: iunit=10, ounit=11
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! Commonly used numbers.
!_______________________________________________________________________

  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: zero = 0.0_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: half = 0.5_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: one  = 1.0_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: two  = 2.0_r_knd

  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: pi = 3.14159265358979_r_knd
!_______________________________________________________________________
!_______________________________________________________________________


END MODULE global_module

```

When the cursor hovers over `r_kind`, all instances of `r_kind` should be highlighted, including those representing `REAL` type suffixes:

#55","user":{"login":"dylon","id":516519,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxNjUxOQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/516519?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon","html_url":"https://github.com/dylon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Changes:
- Addresses issue #55 by jumping to symbol definitions in external documents.
- Resolves the canonical paths of includes.
- Corrects off-by-one errors on changes to the line numbers from lfortran.
- Adds support for matching completions against all symbols in the document.
- Corrects the completion kinds by mapping them from type SymbolKind to type CompletionItemKind.
- Corrects the mtime type of FileCacheEntry to Date; resolves file paths while extracting definitions.
- Corrects highlighting and query extraction logic by matching against type strings in additional to symbol literals.
- Depends on lfortran when extracting hover previews.
- Removes LFortranLanguageServer.renameSymbol since it has been deprecated and replaced with the analogous method of LFortranCLIAccessor.
- Corrects unit and integration tests for latest changes.

To understand the changes to text highlighting and query extraction, consider [global.f90](https://github.com/certik/SNAP/blob/master/src/global.f90) from the [SNAP](https://github.com/certik/SNAP/tree/master) repo:

```fortran
!-----------------------------------------------------------------------
!
! MODULE: global_module
!> @brief
!> Global variables for data types, file names and unit numbers, and
!> commonly used numbers
!
!-----------------------------------------------------------------------

MODULE global_module

  IMPLICIT NONE

  PUBLIC

  SAVE
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! Numerical kinds.
!_______________________________________________________________________

  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: l_knd = KIND( .TRUE. )
  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: i_knd = SELECTED_INT_KIND( 8 )
  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: r_knd = SELECTED_REAL_KIND( 13 )
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! File names and unit numbers.
!
! ifile       - input file name
! iunit       - input file unit number
! ofile       - output file name
! ounit       - output file unit number
!_______________________________________________________________________

  CHARACTER(LEN=64) :: ifile, ofile

  INTEGER(i_knd), PARAMETER :: iunit=10, ounit=11
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! Commonly used numbers.
!_______________________________________________________________________

  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: zero = 0.0_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: half = 0.5_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: one  = 1.0_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: two  = 2.0_r_knd

  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: pi = 3.14159265358979_r_knd
!_______________________________________________________________________
!_______________________________________________________________________


END MODULE global_module

```

When the cursor hovers over `r_kind`, all instances of `r_kind` should be highlighted, including those representing `REAL` type suffixes:

- Addresses issue #55 by jumping to symbol definitions in external documents.
- Resolves the canonical paths of includes.
- Corrects off-by-one errors on changes to the line numbers from lfortran.
- Adds support for matching completions against all symbols in the document.
- Corrects the completion kinds by mapping them from type SymbolKind to type CompletionItemKind.
- Corrects the mtime type of FileCacheEntry to Date; resolves file paths while extracting definitions.
- Corrects highlighting and query extraction logic by matching against type strings in additional to symbol literals.
- Depends on lfortran when extracting hover previews.
- Removes LFortranLanguageServer.renameSymbol since it has been deprecated and replaced with the analogous method of LFortranCLIAccessor.
- Corrects unit and integration tests for latest changes.

To understand the changes to text highlighting and query extraction, consider [global.f90](https://github.com/certik/SNAP/blob/master/src/global.f90) from the [SNAP](https://github.com/certik/SNAP/tree/master) repo:

```fortran
!-----------------------------------------------------------------------
!
! MODULE: global_module
!> @brief
!> Global variables for data types, file names and unit numbers, and
!> commonly used numbers
!
!-----------------------------------------------------------------------

MODULE global_module

  IMPLICIT NONE

  PUBLIC

  SAVE
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! Numerical kinds.
!_______________________________________________________________________

  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: l_knd = KIND( .TRUE. )
  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: i_knd = SELECTED_INT_KIND( 8 )
  INTEGER, PARAMETER :: r_knd = SELECTED_REAL_KIND( 13 )
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! File names and unit numbers.
!
! ifile       - input file name
! iunit       - input file unit number
! ofile       - output file name
! ounit       - output file unit number
!_______________________________________________________________________

  CHARACTER(LEN=64) :: ifile, ofile

  INTEGER(i_knd), PARAMETER :: iunit=10, ounit=11
!_______________________________________________________________________
!
! Commonly used numbers.
!_______________________________________________________________________

  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: zero = 0.0_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: half = 0.5_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: one  = 1.0_r_knd
  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: two  = 2.0_r_knd

  REAL(r_knd), PARAMETER :: pi = 3.14159265358979_r_knd
!_______________________________________________________________________
!_______________________________________________________________________


END MODULE global_module

```

When the cursor hovers over `r_kind`, all instances of `r_kind` should be highlighted, including those representing `REAL` type suffixes:

1. Adds support for code completion with definition previews.
2. Minor bug fixes:
    1. Fixes error where client main script pointed to the wrong location (issue with development artifacts).
    2. Renames the configuration from `"Example configuration"` to `"LFortran Language Server"` (visible in VSCode's settings).
    3. Decrements the column offsets from lfortran by one for correctness.

Notes:
1. Prefix tries are used to index terms for code completion.
2. The final nodes of the prefix tries, representing ending characters of completion terms, map to values of type `CompletionItem` so lookups can be performed quickly.
3. These indices will be used for hover previews and anywhere else they are useful.
4. VSCode does its own filtering atop whatever we return for code completion, so our `onCompletion` handler is not always guaranteed to be called and it is not guaranteed that all the results we return will be presented to the user for completion.
5. We can force VSCode to always call our `onCompletion` handler on keystrokes but cannot prevent it from post-processing (filtering) our changes:
1. Adds support for code completion with definition previews.
2. Minor bug fixes:
    1. Fixes error where client main script pointed to the wrong location (issue with development artifacts).
    2. Renames the configuration from `"Example configuration"` to `"LFortran Language Server"` (visible in VSCode's settings).
    3. Decrements the column offsets from lfortran by one for correctness.

Notes:
1. Prefix tries are used to index terms for code completion.
2. The final nodes of the prefix tries, representing ending characters of completion terms, map to values of type `CompletionItem` so lookups can be performed quickly.
3. These indices will be used for hover previews and anywhere else they are useful.
4. VSCode does its own filtering atop whatever we return for code completion, so our `onCompletion` handler is not always guaranteed to be called and it is not guaranteed that all the results we return will be presented to the user for completion.
5. We can force VSCode to always call our `onCompletion` handler on keystrokes but cannot prevent it from post-processing (filtering) our results.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran-lsp/issues/8/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran-lsp/issues/8/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran-lsp/issues/comments/2484766230","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran-lsp/pull/8#issuecomment-2484766230","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran-lsp/issues/8","id":2484766230,"node_id":"IC_kwDOM4tkcc6UGoYW","user":{"login":"dylon","id":516519,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxNjUxOQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/516519?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon","html_url":"https://github.com/dylon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-19T05:53:27Z","updated_at":"2024-11-19T05:53:27Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> I haven't tested it, but I think we can merge it.

It would best if we could rename them across a project rather than just the current file, but we must be able to rename them within at least the current file.

Support for this will require indexing a symbol's locations within the current file and possibly within its project. We may then use that same index for LSP's various symbol reference functionalities.

Requirements:
1. Need a command-line option, named `--rename-symbol` or the like.
2. It will accept the following parameters:
    1. `--line` := line on which the symbol to rename appears (accepts int param; must be within the respective range)
    2. `--column` := column on which the symbol to rename appears (accepts int param; must be within the respective range)
    3. `--new-name` (or something like that) := what to replace the symbol with (accepts string param; must be valid identifier)
    4. The final, positional parameter will be the path to the file who's symbol should be renamed (as is the current behavior).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5419/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5419/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

protocol"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-11-19T20:08:30Z","updated_at":"2024-11-20T15:15:25Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"For our LSP integration, we will need a command-line option to contextually rename symbols. It would best if we could rename them across a project rather than just the current file, but we must be able to rename them within at least the current file.

Support for this will require indexing a symbol's locations within the current file and possibly within its project. We may then use that same index for LSP's various symbol reference functionalities.

Requirements:
1. Need a command-line option, named `--rename-symbol` or the like.
2. It will accept the following parameters:
    1. `--line` := line on which the symbol to rename appears (accepts int param; must be within the respective range)
    2. `--column` := column on which the symbol to rename appears (accepts int param; must be within the respective range)
    3. `--new-name` (or something like that) := what to replace the symbol with (accepts string param; must be valid identifier)
    4. The final, positional parameter will be the path to the file whose symbol should be renamed (as is the current behavior).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5419/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5419/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2488851653","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5419#issuecomment-2488851653","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5419","id":2488851653,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6UWNzF","user":{"login":"dylon","id":516519,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxNjUxOQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/516519?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon","html_url":"https://github.com/dylon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-20T15:15:23Z","updated_at":"2024-11-20T15:15:23Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> What should be the output --- should it modify the file in-place, or rather return all locations to rename and VSCode does the actual renaming?

Per [this issue](https://github.com/microsoft/language-server-protocol/issues/879), [the specification](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/#textDocument_rename), and [the VSCode rename event handler](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-languageserver-node/blob/main/server/src/common/server.ts#L1295), LSP and VSCode expect the server to respond with a list of ranges (starting and ending coordinates, where each coordinate consists of a line and column) consisting of every occurrence of the symbol to rename, including the text to replace each occurrence with; they do not expect the server to edit the document, probably so the changes can be undone by the client. Each instance should adhere to [the TextEdit specification](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/#textEdit).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2488851653/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

> ```

>     at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:105:5) {
>   code: 'ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE'
> }
> ```
> 
> But the test "passes". Is that expected?
> 
> How is LFortran installed at the CI? I can't find the installation process, but the tests seem to use it?

The error is expected. The tests with errors check how well the server can recover from erroneous output from lfortran. mocha does not swallow stdout or stderr when it runs the tests, so all the logging information gets printed in the tests.

lfortran is not installed as part of the unit tests. I need to add an additional suite of integration tests for it and VSCode. The unit tests are intended to assert that our extension behaves as intended with the expected outputs from lfortran and the expected inputs from VSCode. 1. Adds integration tests for current functionality:
    1. Support for jumping to symbol definitions.
    2. Code completion.
    3. Definition previews in code completion.
    4. Definition previews in mouseover events.
2. Integrates with vscode-extension-tester for Selenium-based, end-to-end integration testing.
3. Adds `./scripts/e2e.sh` script to execute the integration tests.
4. Adds GitHub action to execute the integration tests.
5. Updates the bundling logic to be friendly toward the integration tests (and made it more efficient in the process).
6. Adds support for running end-to-end integration tests in a framebuffer for headless mode (only supports xvfb for now).
7. Resolves linter issues.

TODO:
1. Add support for running the e2e tests on Windows in Github Actions.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525","id":2706518031,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6hUjAP","number":5525,"title":"`--show-errors` is not enabled by default and does not generate valid JSON","user":{"login":"dylon","id":516519,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxNjUxOQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/516519?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon","html_url":"https://github.com/dylon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dylon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-30T02:52:43Z","updated_at":"2024-11-30T02:52:44Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"`--show-errors` is not enabled by default but should be because it is required for the LSP integration. To enable it, you must install rapidjson and then pass the flag `-DWITH_JSON=yes` to cmake.

Also, something has changed that now makes it respond with invalid JSON. If I introduce an error to `tests/function_call1.f90` and call `--show-errors` on it, I get the following:

```shell
./src/bin/lfortran --show-errors tests/function_call1.f90
```

```json
{"uri":"uri","diagnostics":["source":"lpyth","range":{"start":{"line":20,"character":1},"end":{"line":20,"character":5}},"message":"Statement or Declaration expected inside program, found Variable name","severity":0}]}
```

This is invalid for the following reason:

```
parse error: ':' not as part of an object at line 1, column 37
```

If you look at the first element of the `"diagnostics"` field, it wants to treat the array as an object and uses field initialization syntax. It looks like something changed to leave off the opening and closing curly braces for the elements of the diagnostics array.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5525/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.

- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
